revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=奈具神社| 別名=奈具社| 所在地=[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良宮ノ上３５３７－１ | 祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]| 創建=不明| 社格=[[旧村社]]| 例祭=10月11日| 画像=
'''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町にある[[神社]]である。[[加佐郡|丹後加佐郡]]十一座の一社でもある。
==祭神== 現在は[[伊勢神宮]]・外宮の祭神、[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。
==祭礼日== *[[祈年祭]]・4月10日 *[[例祭]]・10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]]・11月23日 ==由緒== [[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 *'''以下 境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]] 奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より''' *昔、丹波の郡比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち 家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが[[竹野郡]]の奈具の社です。
==アクセス== *[[北近畿タンゴ鉄道宮津線]]・[[丹後由良駅]]の北西約900ｍ
==参考文献== * 境内案内板「延喜式内 奈具神社の祭神について」 *「日本人の源流を探る 風土記」 監修:坂本勝
{{DEFAULTSORT:なぐじんじゃ}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の神社]]

{{神社| 名称=奈具神社| 別名=奈具社| 所在地=[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良宮ノ上３５３７－１ | 祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]| 創建=不明| 社格=[[旧村社]]| 例祭=10月11日| 画像= }} '''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町にある[[神社]]である。[[加佐郡|丹後加佐郡]]十一座の一社でもある。
==祭神== 現在は[[伊勢神宮]]・外宮の祭神、[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。
==祭礼日== *[[祈年祭]]・4月10日 *[[例祭]]・10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]]・11月23日 ==由緒== [[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 *'''以下 境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]] 奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より''' *昔、丹波の郡比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち 家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが[[竹野郡]]の奈具の社です。
==アクセス== *[[北近畿タンゴ鉄道宮津線]]・[[丹後由良駅]]の北西約900ｍ
==参考文献== * 境内案内板「延喜式内 奈具神社の祭神について」 *「日本人の源流を探る 風土記」 監修:坂本勝
{{DEFAULTSORT:なぐじんじゃ}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=奈具神社| 別名=奈具社| 所在地=[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良宮ノ上3537-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|15.3|N|135|6|10.2|E|region:JP}} 祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]| 創建=不明| 社格=[[旧村社]]| 例祭=10月11日| 画像= }} '''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町にある[[神社]]である。[[加佐郡|丹後加佐郡]]十一座の一社でもある。
==祭神== 現在は[[伊勢神宮]]・外宮の祭神、[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。
==祭礼日== *[[祈年祭]]・4月10日 *[[例祭]]・10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]]・11月23日 ==由緒== [[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 *'''以下 境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]] 奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より''' *昔、丹波の郡比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち 家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが[[竹野郡]]の奈具の社です。
==アクセス== *[[北近畿タンゴ鉄道宮津線]]・[[丹後由良駅]]の北西約900ｍ
==参考文献== * 境内案内板「延喜式内 奈具神社の祭神について」 *「日本人の源流を探る 風土記」 監修:坂本勝
{{DEFAULTSORT:なぐじんじゃ}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

[[旧村社]]| 例祭=10月11日| 画像= }} '''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町にある[[神社]]である。[[加佐郡|丹後加佐郡]]十一座の一社でもある。 ==祭神== 現在は[[伊勢神宮]]・外宮の祭神、[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。 ==祭礼日== *[[祈年祭]]・4月10日 *[[例祭]]・10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]]・11月23日 ==由緒== [[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 *'''以下　境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]]　奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より''' *昔、丹波の郡比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 　　天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち　家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが[[竹野郡]]の奈具の社です。 ==アクセス== *[[北近畿タンゴ鉄道宮津線]]・[[丹後由良駅]]の北西約900ｍ ==参考文献== * 境内案内板「延喜式内　奈具神社の祭神について」 *「日本人の源流を探る　風土記」　監修:坂本勝 {{DEFAULTSORT:なぐじんじゃ}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{神社 |名称=奈具神社 |別名=奈具社 |画像=[[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良宮ノ上3537-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]] |創建=不明 |社格=[[式内社]](小)
[[旧村社]] |例祭=10月11日 }} '''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]。[[加佐郡|丹後加佐郡]]十一座の一社でもある。 == 祭神 == [[伊勢神宮]]外宮の祭神である[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。 * [[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]] == 祭礼日 == *[[祈年祭]] - 4月10日 *[[例祭]] - 10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]] - 11月23日 == 由緒 == [[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 {{quotation| 昔、丹波の郡比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち　家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが[[竹野郡 (京都府)|竹野郡]]の奈具の社です。|境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]]　奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より}} == アクセス == * [[京都丹後鉄道]][[京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|宮舞線]][[丹後由良駅]]から北西約900m == 参考文献 == {{No footnotes|date=2020年6月|section=1}} * 境内案内板「延喜式内 奈具神社の祭神について」 * [[坂本勝 (文学者)|坂本勝]]（監修）『図説 日本人の源流を探る 風土記』青春出版社、2008年 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なくしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宮津市の建築物]] [[Category:宮津市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称=奈具神社 |別名=奈具社 |画像=[[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良宮ノ上3537-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]] |創建=不明 |社格=[[式内社]](小)
[[旧村社]] |例祭=10月11日 }} '''奈具神社'''（なぐじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[宮津市]]由良にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、[[丹後国]][[加佐郡]]十一座の一社。 == 祭神 == [[伊勢神宮]]外宮の祭神である[[豊受大神]]と同一で、[[羽衣]]伝承の[[天女]]でもある[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]]が[[祭神]]である。 * [[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能賣命]] == 祭礼日 == *[[祈年祭]] - 4月10日 *[[例祭]] - 10月10日 *[[新嘗祭]] - 11月23日 == 由緒 == 京都府[[京丹後市]][[弥栄町]]船木に同名の奈具神社があり、こちらも式内社で、丹後国[[竹野郡 (京都府)|竹野郡]]十四座の一社である。[[丹後国風土記]]によると「ここに来りてわが心奈具志久（なぐしく）なれり」とあり、奈具神社の由来はこの奈具志久（おだやかに）という言葉による。 {{quotation| 昔、[[中郡 (京都府)|丹波の郡]]比治の真奈井に天下った天女が、和奈佐の老夫婦に懇願されて比治の里にとどまり、万病に効くという酒を醸して、老夫婦は莫大な富を得ました。しかし、悪念を抱いた老夫婦はやがて天女に、 汝は吾が子ではないと追い出してしまいました。 天の原ふりさけみれば霞立ち　家路まどいて行方しらずも と詠い、比治の里を退き村々を遍歴の果てに、舟木の里の奈具の村にやってきました。 そして「此処にして我が心なぐしく成りぬ」（わたしの心は安らかになりました）と云って、この村を安住の地としました。 此処で終焉を迎えた[[天女]]は村人たちによって、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇賀能売命]]（とようかのめのみこと）として祀られました。 これが竹野郡の奈具の社です。|境内案内板「[[延喜式神名帳|延喜式内]]　奈具神社の[[祭神]]について」より}} == アクセス == * [[京都丹後鉄道]][[京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|宮舞線]][[丹後由良駅]]から北西約900m == 参考文献 == {{No footnotes|date=2020年6月|section=1}} * 境内案内板「延喜式内 奈具神社の祭神について」 * [[坂本勝 (文学者)|坂本勝]]（監修）『図説 日本人の源流を探る 風土記』青春出版社、2008年 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なくしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宮津市の建築物]] [[Category:宮津市の歴史]]

奈具神社 [[Category:奈具神社]][[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |Image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |Location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} |Coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |Deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == Deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the Deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the Deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[ja:奈具神社]]

Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[ja:奈具神社]]

奈具神社 [[Category:奈具神社]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Nagu Shrine]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[ja:奈具神社]] {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. {{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}}

{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}}

This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}}

She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. 奈具神社 [[Category:奈具神社]][[Category:11]] [[en:Nagu Shrine]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[ja:奈具神社]] {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

奈具神社 [[Category:奈具神社]][[Category:11]] [[en:Nagu Shrine]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[ja:奈具神社]] {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[en:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
奈具神社 {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=draft}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡|12=draft}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=draft}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival|12=draft}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=draft}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|12=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:奈具神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}}

{{ill|Former village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|ja|宮津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=draft|12=draft}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival|12=draft|12=draft}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=draft|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|12=draft|12=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=draft|12=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:奈具神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=draft|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|12=draft|12=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=draft|12=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. This shrine is also a Shikinai-sha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=draft}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|12=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagu Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:奈具神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]]

After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft}}. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:奈具神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]]

However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Tango Province|ja|加佐郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa District, Tango Province|qq=}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyotango, Kyoto|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyotango, Kyoto|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|qq=}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|ja|丹後由良駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango-Yura Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]]

{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|qq=}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and one of the eleven shrines of [[:en:Kasa District, Kyoto|Kasa District, Tango Province]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango, Kyoto]]. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto
revision.text:
奈具神社 {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|qq=}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and one of the eleven shrines of [[:en:Kasa District, Kyoto|Kasa District, Tango Province]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango, Kyoto]]. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[en:Nagu Shrine]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:宮津市の歴史]]

奈具神社 {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|qq=}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and one of the eleven shrines of [[:en:Kasa District, Kyoto|Kasa District, Tango Province]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango, Kyoto]]. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto�]]

This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. revision.text:
奈具神社 {{Shrine |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = [[File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg|300px]] |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|35|31|14.3|N|135|16|17.2|E|region:JP}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Annual Festival = October 11 }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Yura, [[:en:Miyazu, Kyoto|Miyazu, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and one of the eleven shrines of [[:en:Kasa District, Kyoto|Kasa District, Tango Province]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango, Kyoto]]. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagu Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]]

It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of [[:en:Kasa District, Kyoto|Kasa District, Tango Province]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|qq=}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|qq=}}, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango, Kyoto]]. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of [[:en:Tango-Yura Station|Tango-Yura Station]] on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:奈具神社]] [[sn:Nagu Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]]
