[[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11444944]] Wikidata name: Nagu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 奈具神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.520639|N|135.271444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = October 11 |location = {{ill|Miyazu|WD=Q846351}} }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Kyoto|en|Kasa District, Kyoto|WD=Q6373931|lt=Kasa District, Tango Province}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|1=tennyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111467505}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|1=Yasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487446}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyotango, Kyoto}}. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|1=Takeno district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599473}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|en|Tango-Yura Station|WD=Q7683073|lt=Tango-Yura Station}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|1=Miyamai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125224}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奈具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444944}} [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] 奈具神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.520639|N|135.271444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = October 11 |location = {{ill|Miyazu|WD=Q846351}} }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Kyoto|en|Kasa District, Kyoto|WD=Q6373931|lt=Kasa District, Tango Province}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|1=tennyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111467505}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|1=Yasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487446}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyotango, Kyoto}}. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|1=Takeno district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599473}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|en|Tango-Yura Station|WD=Q7683073|lt=Tango-Yura Station}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|1=Miyamai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125224}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奈具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444944}} [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] 奈具神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.520639|N|135.271444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = October 11 |location = {{ill|Miyazu|WD=Q846351}} }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Kyoto|en|Kasa District, Kyoto|WD=Q6373931|lt=Kasa District, Tango Province}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|1=tennyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111467505}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|1=Yasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487446}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyotango, Kyoto}}. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|1=Takeno district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599473}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|en|Tango-Yura Station|WD=Q7683073|lt=Tango-Yura Station}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|1=Miyamai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125224}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Willer Trains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奈具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444944}} [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Qq]]

{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = October 11 |location = {{ill|Miyazu|WD=Q846351|1=Miyazu, Kyoto}} }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto|1=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Kyoto|en|Kasa District, Kyoto|WD=Q6373931|lt=Kasa District, Tango Province|1=Kasa District, Kyoto}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|1=Celestial maiden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111467505}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|1=Yasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487446}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyotango, Kyoto|1=Kyōtango}}. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|1=Takeno district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599473}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|en|Tango-Yura Station|WD=Q7683073|lt=Tango-Yura Station|1=Tango-Yura Station}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|1=Miyamai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125224}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Kyoto Tango Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奈具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444944}} [[Category:Buildings in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] [[ja:奈具神社]] 奈具神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nagu Shrine |Other Name = Nagu-sha |image = File:Nagu-jinja torii.jpg |location = 3537-1 Miyanoe, Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto|1=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.520639|N|135.271444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} |Established = Unknown |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Former Village shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = October 11 |location = {{ill|Miyazu|WD=Q846351|1=Miyazu, Kyoto}} }} '''Nagu Shrine''' (奈具神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yura, {{ill|Miyazu, Kyoto|en|Miyazu, Kyoto|WD=Q846351|lt=Miyazu, Kyoto|1=Miyazu, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and one of the eleven shrines of {{ill|Kasa District, Kyoto|en|Kasa District, Kyoto|WD=Q6373931|lt=Kasa District, Tango Province|1=Kasa District, Kyoto}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, also known as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is identified with the celestial maiden ({{ill|Tennyo|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennyo|1=Celestial maiden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111467505}}) from the {{ill|Hagoromo legend|ja|羽衣伝承|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagoromo legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} - April 10 * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} - October 10 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23 == History == There is another shrine with the same name, Nagu Shrine, located in {{ill|Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|ja|弥栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funaki, Yasaka, Kyotango|1=Yasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487446}}, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyotango, Kyoto|1=Kyōtango}}. This shrine is also a Shikinaisha and one of the fourteen shrines of {{ill|Takeno District, Tango Province|ja|竹野郡 (京都府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeno District, Tango Province|1=Takeno district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599473}}. According to the {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}}, the name "Nagu" derives from the word "Nagusiku" (meaning "to be at peace"), referring to the peaceful heart of the deity. {{quotation| Long ago, a celestial maiden descended to the sacred well of Hiji in the district of {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波の郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She was persuaded by an elderly couple, Wanana and Sana, to remain in the village of Hiji, where she brewed sake believed to cure all ailments, bringing great wealth to the couple. However, the couple, driven by greed, eventually cast her out, declaring, "You are not our child." She composed the following poem: ''"Looking up at the vast heavens, shrouded in mist, I have lost my way and do not know my path."'' After leaving Hiji, she wandered through many villages until she arrived at the village of Nagu in Funaki. There she declared, ''"Here, my heart has found peace (nagusiku),"'' and decided to settle. After her passing, the villagers enshrined her as {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊宇賀能賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This is the origin of Nagu Shrine in Takeno District. |From the shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nagu Shrine"}} == Access == * Approximately 900 meters northwest of {{ill|Tango-Yura Station|en|Tango-Yura Station|WD=Q7683073|lt=Tango-Yura Station|1=Tango-Yura Station}} on the {{ill|Miyamai Line|ja|京都丹後鉄道宮舞線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamai Line|1=Miyamai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125224}}, {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|ja|京都丹後鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Tango Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to WILLER TRAINS|1=Kyoto Tango Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19727758}}. == References == {{No footnotes|date=June 2020|section=1}} * Shrine grounds information board: "About the deity of the Shikinai Nagu Shrine" * {{ill|Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja|坂本勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Masaru (literary scholar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Supervision). *Illustrated Exploration of the Origins of the Japanese People: Fudoki*. Seishun Publishing, 2008. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈具神社|version=103400268|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奈具神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444944}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Miyazu, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
